complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down into research Firestore best practices, draft initial schema tree, create detailed field specifications table, add validation rules and examples, document indexing strategy and query patterns.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Straightforward documentation task with detailed specifications provided. Low technical risk, follows Firestore best practices from search results[1][2]. Already has 5 well-defined subtasks covering all aspects.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Setup Firebase Project and Local Emulator
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Separate into Firebase account setup/authentication, project creation and service enabling, CLI configuration and .firebaserc setup, emulator installation and port configuration, storage rules initialization and initial testing.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Standard Firebase setup with multiple CLI commands and configuration steps. Potential port conflicts and version compatibility issues. Dependencies on Task 1. Already has comprehensive 5 subtasks.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement Firestore and Storage Security Rules
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide into Firestore rules syntax validation, Storage rules size/contentType validation, rules file creation and syntax checking, deployment process, comprehensive Rules Playground testing scenarios.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Complex security rules with field validation, size limits, and content type checking. Requires deep Firebase rules knowledge and thorough testing. Critical for data security. Well-covered by 5 subtasks.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Split into FlutterFire CLI installation, project configuration and firebase_options generation, pubspec dependency updates, main.dart Firebase initialization, multi-platform verification (android/ios/web).
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Standard Flutter Firebase integration following official patterns. Multiple moving parts (CLI, config generation, platform setup). Dependencies on Tasks 2,3. Good 5-subtask breakdown.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Extend Domain Layer - ArticleEntity and Repository
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Separate ArticleEntity field additions, copyWith method implementation with full field coverage, repository interface method additions with proper typing, Dart analyzer validation, unit test coverage.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Clean Architecture domain layer extension. Requires careful null safety and copyWith pattern maintenance. Pure Dart, highly testable. Dependencies on Task 4. Solid 5 subtasks.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break into individual use case implementations (UploadArticle, UploadThumbnail, GetUserArticles), Params classes creation, DI container registration, comprehensive unit testing for each.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Standard Clean Architecture use cases following established patterns. Three separate implementations with testing. Dependency injection wiring. Dependencies on Task 5. Well-structured 5 subtasks.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement Data Sources - Firestore and Storage
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide into interface definitions, Firestore data source implementation with error handling, Storage data source with UUID generation and file handling, integration testing setup with emulators.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: First Firebase SDK integration in data layer. File I/O, UUID generation, async error handling, emulator integration testing. High complexity due to external dependencies. Excellent 5-subtask coverage.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Separate fromFirestore factory with DocumentSnapshot handling, toFirestore with FieldValue.serverTimestamp logic, fromEntity/toEntity bidirectional mapping, comprehensive roundtrip unit tests.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Complex serialization with Firestore-specific types (DocumentSnapshot, FieldValue.serverTimestamp). Null safety, bidirectional mapping, testing critical. Dependencies on 5,7. Thorough 5 subtasks.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break into constructor DI wiring, individual method implementations (uploadArticle, uploadThumbnail, getUserArticles), exception mapping strategies, comprehensive unit tests with mocked data sources.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Repository pattern implementation bridging domain and data layers. Complex error handling (FirebaseException → DataFailed), model/entity conversion. Dependencies on 6,7,8. Detailed 5 subtasks.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Separate package version verification, data source registrations, use case registrations with dependency resolution, DI resolution testing, documentation for future cubit registrations.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: GetIt DI container setup following established patterns. Risk of circular dependencies or resolution failures. Dependencies on Task 9. Clean 5-subtask structure.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide into state classes definition, cubit skeleton with DI, thumbnail-first upload orchestration logic, article modification with copyWith, complete error handling and state flow testing.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Complex BLoC/Cubit orchestration: thumbnail → article sequencing, state management, error propagation. Business logic coordination. Dependencies on 6,10. Sophisticated 5-subtask breakdown.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Separate state hierarchy with Equatable, cubit structure and DI, fetchUserArticles with Either folding, DI registration, comprehensive bloc_test/widget testing coverage.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Standard list-fetching BLoC with Either<DataState> handling. Less complex than upload flow but requires proper state management patterns. Dependencies on 6,10. Good 5 subtasks.
